message:
The other day, I was rummaging through our family go-bags and came across a deck of cards I had stashed away. It hit me—when was the last time we actually played a game together? With life buzzing around us, we often forget the simple joys. So, we decided to have a little family game night, no screens involved. Just laughter, some friendly competition, and a reminder that sometimes it's the low-tech fun that brings us closer. 

While we were at it, I made sure to review our emergency plans and go-bags so we’re all set for anything that might come our way. It felt good to blend preparedness with a bit of joy. Have you rediscovered any low-tech fun lately?
